codeReview.summary: The feature covers the required Conversations CRUD and item create/retrieve/list/delete routes, local blank-id guards, typed delete/list envelopes, and live-smoke evidence for persistence. I found one non-blocking typing gap: conversation items expose fewer typed fields than the existing Responses output item surface and the official conversation-item contract, so known tool-call/reasoning fields fall back to `extra`.

issues.description: `ConversationItem` only exposes `id`, `type`, `role`, `status`, `content`, and `extra`, whereas conversation items are documented as the same family of item types as Responses output items. Compared with `src/resources/responses.rs:495`, standard fields such as `name`, `arguments`, `input`, `code`, and `call_id` are not surfaced on the typed Conversations item model, so callers retrieving/listing tool-call or reasoning items must dig through `extra` instead of using typed accessors.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `sdk-surface-worker` skill still assumes an interactive brainstorming approval gate, but mission workers run in exec mode and cannot pause for approval. This worker explicitly had to deviate from the written procedure for that reason.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/sdk-surface-worker/SKILL.md:16-25 requires `brainstorming` before public API changes; the handoff at /Users/staticpayload/.factory/missions/beaa247f-5975-4da4-acc2-19deef388841/handoffs/2026-04-05T17-32-31-106Z__conversations-crud-and-items__0638075d-2ae3-44b2-9cb2-283e7b07d9ba.json:91-100 records `followedProcedure=false` because the worker could not honor the brainstorming approval gate in exec mode.

summary: Pass. The implementation satisfies VAL-RESP-011 and VAL-RESP-012 with matching CRUD/item routes, local guards, pagination helpers, and live persistence evidence. The only issue I found is a non-blocking ergonomics/typing gap in `ConversationItem`, plus one actionable skill-gap observation about the brainstorming approval gate in exec mode.
